name: Telegram Task Assistant
description: One-message automation: Telegram trigger starts a multi-agent process that executes a task, formats result, and replies back to the same chat.

data.goal: Watch incoming Telegram Bot messages and start this workflow when a new private text task arrives. Pass trigger runtime payload downstream.

Execute the user task from trigger payload end-to-end.

Read Trigger runtime input JSON from KOVALSKY_TRIGGER_INPUT_JSON.
Extract task text from first non-empty field:
- payload.message.text
- payload.update.message.text
- payload.text
- payload.reason

Execution rules:
- start immediately, no extra confirmations if task is clear
- use browser/tools when needed
- save any created artifacts in ./task-output

Output:
- save report to ./task-output/execution_result.md with:
  - received task
  - actions performed
  - key outputs (files/links)
  - final status

Convert execution output into a clean user-facing reply package.

Inputs:
- ./task-output/execution_result.md
- files in ./task-output created by previous step

Output:
- save JSON to ./task-output/reply_payload.json with:
  {
    "summary": "...",
    "details_markdown": "...",
    "artifacts": [{"path":"...","note":"..."}],
    "status": "success|partial|failed"
  }
- save final reply text to ./task-output/reply_message.md

Send the prepared reply back to the same Telegram chat that triggered this run.

Inputs:
- ./task-output/reply_message.md
- ./task-output/reply_payload.json
- Trigger runtime input JSON (KOVALSKY_TRIGGER_INPUT_JSON)

Find chat id from first non-empty field:
- payload.message.chat.id
- payload.update.message.chat.id
- payload.chat_id

Send ./task-output/reply_message.md to that chat via Telegram integration configured in gateway.

Output:
- save delivery log to ./task-output/delivery_status.md with chat_id, send status, and any error details.
